Notice Of Motion In Support Of Motion To Seal Declaration In Support Of Request For Dismissal
Off Calendar Dec-21-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT OMNI HOTELS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION'S Motion To Seal Declaration In Support Of Request For Dismissal.

Defendant's unopposed motion to seal is granted. The proposed order shall state the factors in CRC 2.550(d).

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-25-2022 Continued To Feb-15-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Request For Dismissal Without Prejudice Pursuant To C.R.C. 3.770
Off Calendar Oct-06-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DAVID ARAKELYAN, Esq. of Setareh Law Group (Tel: 310-888-7771) for plaintiff Evelyn Chantell LaChapelle;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF EVELYN LACHAPELLE's Request For Dismissal Without Prejudice Pursuant To C.R.C. 3.770.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is denied without prejudice. CRC 3.770(a) provides that the moving declaration "must clearly state whether consideration, direct or indirect, is being given for the dismissal" Here, counsel's cryptic declaration states: "Counsel is unaware whether any consideration has been paid for this dismissal." (Keledjian Decl., par. 7.) That declaration does not meet the requirement of the rule.

Parties to prepare and submit a proposed order tracking the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/RBU)
